description: MatSlop - Open-source MATLAB alternative IDE built with Electron, React, TypeScript, and GNU Octave

userStories.title: Electron App Scaffold and Build System
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the base Electron + React + TypeScript project scaffolded with a working build system so that all subsequent features have a foundation.
acceptanceCriteria: Electron app launches with a blank React-rendered window
TypeScript compilation works with strict mode enabled
Hot-reload works in development mode (npm run dev)
ESLint and Prettier configured with reasonable defaults
Package.json includes scripts: dev, build, lint, typecheck
Typecheck passes
Lint passes

userStories.title: Main Window Layout with Resizable Panel System
userStories.description: As a user, I want a MATLAB-like IDE layout with resizable panels so I can arrange my workspace comfortably.
acceptanceCriteria: Main window has four panel areas: Editor (center), Command Window (bottom), Workspace (top-right), File Browser (left sidebar)
Panels are resizable by dragging dividers between them
Panels can be collapsed and expanded
Minimum panel sizes prevent panels from being dragged to zero width/height
Layout uses a split-pane library (e.g., allotment or react-resizable-panels)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Monaco Editor with MATLAB Syntax Highlighting
userStories.description: As a user, I want a code editor with MATLAB syntax highlighting so I can write .m files comfortably.
acceptanceCriteria: Monaco Editor embedded in the Editor panel area

Custom MATLAB/Octave language definition registered with keywords (if, else, elseif, for, while, function, end, switch, case, try, catch, classdef, return, break, continue), comment syntax (% line, %{ block %}), string literals ('...' and "..."), number literals, operators, matrix brackets
Line numbers displayed
Code folding for function/end, if/end, for/end, while/end blocks
Multiple editor tabs supported - clicking a tab switches the active file
Tab shows filename with dot indicator for unsaved changes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: File Open, Save, Save As, and New File
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create, open, and save .m files so I can manage my scripts.
acceptanceCriteria: New File action creates a new untitled tab in the editor
Open File shows native file dialog filtered to .m files and opens the selected file in a new tab
Save writes current tab contents to disk (prompts for path if untitled)
Save As prompts for new path and saves
Ctrl+S triggers save, Ctrl+Shift+S triggers Save As
Closing a tab with unsaved changes prompts a Save/Discard/Cancel dialog
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: File Browser Panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want a file browser panel showing the current working directory so I can navigate and open files.
acceptanceCriteria: File Browser panel on the left shows a directory tree of the current working directory
Directories are expandable/collapsible with arrow icons
Files show appropriate icons (special icon for .m files)
Double-clicking a .m file opens it in the editor
Right-click context menu with: Open, Rename, Delete, New File, New Folder
Current directory path shown at top with a Change Directory button that opens a folder picker
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Octave Path Auto-Detection and Configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the app to find the GNU Octave installation automatically or let the user configure it.
acceptanceCriteria: On startup, searches for octave-cli or octave binary in: /usr/bin/octave, /usr/local/bin/octave, /opt/homebrew/bin/octave, common Windows paths (C:\Program Files\GNU Octave\*\bin\octave-cli.exe), and PATH
If found, stores path in electron-store config
If NOT found, shows dialog: 'GNU Octave not found' with instructions and a Browse button for manual selection
Settings allow changing the Octave path at any time
Validates path points to a working Octave binary before accepting (runs octave --version)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: GNU Octave Backend Process Manager
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a backend service that manages a GNU Octave child process so the app can execute MATLAB-compatible commands.
acceptanceCriteria: On app startup (after Octave path is configured), spawns GNU Octave with --no-gui --interactive --no-history flags
Can send commands to Octave via stdin and capture stdout/stderr output
Uses a unique delimiter/prompt marker to detect when command output is complete
Handles Octave process crashes gracefully with error notification and restart option
Can interrupt running commands by sending SIGINT to the Octave process
Properly terminates Octave process on app quit
Exposes IPC API from main process to renderer: executeCommand(cmd) returns {output, error, isComplete}
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Command Window (Terminal Panel)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a command window where I can type MATLAB commands interactively, just like MATLAB's Command Window.
acceptanceCriteria: Command Window panel displayed at the bottom of the IDE
Shows >> prompt for input
User can type commands and press Enter to execute via Octave backend
Command output displayed below the input preserving formatting
Error messages displayed in red text
Command history accessible with Up/Down arrow keys
Supports multi-line input with ... continuation
Can interrupt running command with Ctrl+C
Scrollable output with auto-scroll to bottom on new output
clc command clears the command window
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Status Bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want a status bar showing engine status, cursor position, and current directory.
acceptanceCriteria: Status bar at the bottom of the window below all panels
Shows current working directory on the left side
Shows Octave engine status: Ready (green), Busy (yellow), or Disconnected (red) with colored dot indicator
Shows cursor position (line:column) for the active editor tab
Shows file encoding (UTF-8)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Editor Toolbar with Run Controls
userStories.description: As a user, I want a toolbar above the editor with buttons for common actions like run and stop.
acceptanceCriteria: Toolbar row above the editor with icon buttons: New File, Open File, Save, Run (green play), Stop (red square), Run Section
Buttons have tooltips showing name and keyboard shortcut
Run button is disabled when no file is open
Stop button is disabled when Octave is not busy, enabled when busy
Uses Lucide React icons for consistent style
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Run .m Script from Editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run the current .m script and see output in the command window.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking Run button or pressing F5 runs the current file
Running a script auto-saves the file first
Script executes via Octave backend with output appearing in Command Window
Current working directory is set to the script's directory before execution
Run Section (Ctrl+Enter) runs only the code in the current cell delimited by %% markers
Errors show file:line references in the Command Window output
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: MATLAB-Compatible Current Directory Management
userStories.description: As a user, I want cd, pwd, ls, dir commands to work and keep the file browser in sync.
acceptanceCriteria: cd command in Command Window changes Octave's working directory AND updates File Browser panel and status bar
pwd returns the current directory
ls and dir list directory contents in the Command Window
Changing directory via File Browser's Change Directory button also updates Octave's cd and the status bar
addpath and rmpath work for managing the Octave search path
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Workspace / Variable Viewer Panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all variables in my workspace with names, sizes, types, and value previews.
acceptanceCriteria: Workspace panel shows a table with columns: Name, Value (preview), Size, Class
After each command execution, workspace refreshes by querying Octave (whos command)
Scalar values show their value directly in the Value column
Vectors/matrices show dimensions (e.g., '3x3 double')
Strings show a truncated preview
Structs show field count, cell arrays show dimensions
Variables are sorted alphabetically by default
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Variable Inspector Dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want to double-click a variable to view and edit its full contents in a spreadsheet-like view.
acceptanceCriteria: Double-clicking a numeric matrix/vector in workspace opens a modal with a spreadsheet-style grid showing values
Grid cells are editable; editing a cell sends an assignment command to Octave to update the variable
Struct variables show fields in a key-value list view
String variables show full text in a text area
Dialog title shows variable name, size, and class
Close button returns to main view
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Command History Panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want a searchable command history so I can recall and re-run previous commands.
acceptanceCriteria: Command History panel toggleable from View menu
Shows all commands executed in current and previous sessions
Double-clicking a history entry pastes it into the Command Window input
Search/filter box at top of history panel filters the list
History persists across app restarts via a local file
Right-click context menu: Copy, Execute, Delete from History
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Plot/Figure Window Support
userStories.description: As a user, I want plotting commands to display figures, just like MATLAB.
acceptanceCriteria: plot(), scatter(), bar(), histogram() commands produce visible figures in the app
Octave renders plots to temporary PNG files using print('-dpng', tmpfile) and the app displays them
Each figure opens in its own tab within a Figures panel or as a floating window
Figures can be resized
Figure toolbar with: Save as Image (PNG/SVG/PDF) button
title(), xlabel(), ylabel(), legend() annotations render correctly
subplot() layouts work correctly
hold on / hold off for overlaying plots works
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Application Menu Bar
userStories.description: As a user, I want a standard application menu bar with all expected IDE menu options.
acceptanceCriteria: File menu: New Script, New Live Script, Open, Save, Save As, Close Tab, Exit
Edit menu: Undo, Redo, Cut, Copy, Paste, Find & Replace, Go to Line, Select All
View menu: Toggle Command Window, Toggle Workspace, Toggle File Browser, Toggle Command History, Toggle Status Bar, Reset Layout
Run menu: Run Script (F5), Run Section (Ctrl+Enter), Stop Execution, Clear Command Window
Help menu: About MatSlop, Octave Documentation (opens web link)
All menu items show keyboard shortcuts
Menu items are disabled when not applicable
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Theme Support (Light and Dark Mode)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to switch between light and dark themes.
acceptanceCriteria: App ships with a light theme (MATLAB-inspired white/blue) and a dark theme
Theme toggle in View menu: Light / Dark / System
All panels, editor, menus, and dialogs styled consistently per theme via CSS variables
Monaco editor theme switches between vs-light and vs-dark accordingly
Theme preference persists across restarts in config
Default follows system preference
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
userStories.description: As a user, I want standard keyboard shortcuts matching MATLAB conventions.
acceptanceCriteria: F5: Run script
Ctrl+C in Command Window: Interrupt execution
Ctrl+Enter: Run current section/cell
Ctrl+N: New file, Ctrl+O: Open, Ctrl+S: Save, Ctrl+W: Close tab
Ctrl+F: Find, Ctrl+H: Find and Replace, Ctrl+G: Go to line
Ctrl+/: Toggle comment on selected lines (add/remove % prefix)
Tab/Shift+Tab: Indent/unindent selected lines
All shortcuts handled via a centralized shortcut manager to avoid conflicts
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Auto-Complete for MATLAB/Octave Functions
userStories.description: As a user, I want auto-complete suggestions for built-in and user-defined functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Typing in editor triggers auto-complete popup after 2+ characters
Suggestions include a comprehensive list of Octave built-in functions (sin, cos, plot, zeros, ones, eye, linspace, etc.)
Suggestions include variable names from the current workspace (fetched from Octave)
Suggestions include function names from .m files in the current directory
Selecting a suggestion inserts it at cursor
Function signatures shown in detail panel (e.g., plot(X, Y, LineSpec))
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Basic Syntax Error Highlighting in Editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want basic syntax errors highlighted before I run my code.
acceptanceCriteria: Unclosed brackets/parentheses underlined in red
Mismatched function/end blocks highlighted
Error messages shown on hover over underlined code via Monaco markers
Diagnostics update as user types with ~500ms debounce
Error count shown in status bar
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Preferences/Settings Dialog
userStories.description: As a user, I want a preferences dialog to customize editor behavior and settings.
acceptanceCriteria: Preferences dialog accessible from Edit > Preferences menu
Settings: Font family and font size for editor
Settings: Tab size (2/4/8 spaces) and spaces vs tabs toggle
Settings: Theme selection (Light/Dark/System)
Settings: Octave executable path with Browse button
Settings: Default working directory
All settings persisted to electron-store config
Changes apply immediately without restart
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Live Script - Basic Cell Structure and File Format
userStories.description: As a user, I want to create live scripts with code and text cells, saved in a JSON-based .mls format.
acceptanceCriteria: File > New Live Script creates a .mls file opened in notebook mode (distinct from the Monaco editor view)
Notebook view shows an ordered list of cells, each either Code or Markdown type
Code cells use Monaco editor instances with MATLAB syntax highlighting
Text/Markdown cells show rendered markdown when not focused, and a markdown editor when focused
Add Cell button (+) between cells to insert new Code or Markdown cell
Delete button on each cell to remove it
File saved as JSON .mls format: {cells: [{type, content, output}]}
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Live Script - Code Cell Execution and Inline Output
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run code cells in a live script and see output inline below the cell.
acceptanceCriteria: Each code cell has a Run Cell button (play icon)
Clicking Run Cell executes the code via Octave and displays text output inline below the cell
Error output shown in red below the cell
Run All button at the top executes all code cells sequentially top-to-bottom
Cell execution order matters - variables persist between cells in the same Octave session
Output is cached in the .mls file so it persists when reopening
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Live Script - Inline Plot Rendering
userStories.description: As a user, I want plots in live script code cells to render inline below the cell.
acceptanceCriteria: When a code cell contains a plot command, the figure renders as an inline image below the cell
Uses Octave's print command to export figure to temp PNG, then displays as an element
Multiple plots in one cell all render below
Inline plots are displayed at a reasonable default size
Right-click on inline plot: Save As Image, Open in Figure Window
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Live Script - Cell Reordering via Drag and Drop
userStories.description: As a user, I want to reorder cells in a live script by dragging them.
acceptanceCriteria: Each cell has a drag handle on the left side
Cells can be reordered via drag-and-drop
Drop indicator shows where the cell will be placed
Reordering updates the .mls file structure
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Find and Replace in Editor
userStories.description: As a user, I want find and replace functionality with regex support.
acceptanceCriteria: Ctrl+F opens find bar in current editor tab (using Monaco's built-in find widget)
Ctrl+H opens find-and-replace mode
Options: Case sensitive, Whole word, Regex toggles
Replace one and Replace all buttons
Match count displayed (e.g., 3 of 12)
Esc closes find bar
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Drag-and-Drop File Opening
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag .m files from my file manager into the editor to open them.
acceptanceCriteria: Dragging a .m file onto the editor area opens it in a new tab
Dragging a .mls file opens it in live script mode
Visual drop indicator (overlay/highlight) shown when dragging over the editor area
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Welcome Tab on First Launch
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a welcome tab with getting-started information on first launch.
acceptanceCriteria: On first launch (no previous config detected), a Welcome to MatSlop tab opens in the editor area
Welcome tab shows: app name/logo, version, quick-start tips, link to Octave documentation
Don't show again checkbox that persists the preference
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Layout State Persistence
userStories.description: As a user, I want my panel sizes and visibility to persist across app restarts.
acceptanceCriteria: Panel sizes (divider positions) saved to electron-store config on change
Panel visibility (collapsed/expanded) saved to config
On app launch, layout restored from saved config
View > Reset Layout menu item restores default layout
Recently opened files list persisted and shown in File menu under Recent Files
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Cross-Platform Build and Packaging
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the app to produce distributable packages for Linux, macOS, and Windows.
acceptanceCriteria: electron-builder configured in package.json for all three platforms
npm run build:linux produces AppImage and/or .deb
npm run build:mac produces .dmg
npm run build:win produces .exe installer (NSIS)
App icon configured for all platforms (can be a placeholder icon initially)
App name MatSlop and version from package.json used in build output
Typecheck passes
